event: Carnival in St. Mark's Square in Venice
symbols: masks and costumes — concealment of identity and the revelry preceding Lenten penitence
gilded interior of St. Mark's Basilica — the sacred space temporarily given over to festivity
dense crowd — the communal excess of Carnival before the austerity of Lent
warm golden light — the opulence of Venetian culture
description: A densely packed interior scene showing a great crowd of figures in carnival costumes and masks gathered inside what appears to be St. Mark's Basilica in Venice. The grand interior features gilded mosaic arches and vaults. The crowd includes figures in elaborate period dress — women in full gowns, men in capes and masks. The atmosphere is one of animated socializing and festive movement. The painting is executed in warm, golden tones with a freely brushed, almost impressionistic quality that captures the energy and chaos of the carnival gathering.

_meta.title: Carnival in St. Mark's Square in Venice
